 {"id":"gt-ni6a","title":"Witness role template + CLAUDE.md","description":"Create the Witness agent's role context:

1. internal/templates/roles/witness.md.tmpl
   - Witness responsibilities (polecat lifecycle)
   - Core loop description
   - Commands available (gt polecats, bd ready, etc)
   - Escalation protocol

2. Generate CLAUDE.md for witness/ directories
   - Context about the rig
   - Mail address: <rig>/witness
   - Startup protocol (gt prime, check inbox)

The Witness is the per-rig 'pit boss' - NOT a global coordinator. It manages polecats for ONE rig only.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:18.617496-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:54:13.697998-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T03:54:13.697998-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-ni6a","depends_on_id":"gt-53w6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:37.105264-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-njem","title":"Remove gt mail dependency on bd mail commands","description":"Replaced bd mail send/inbox/read/ack with bd create/list/show/close. Messages are now stored as beads issues with type=message.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:52:43.47722-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:52:48.155447-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T17:52:48.155447-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-njr","title":"Engineer session restart protocol","description":"Implement session restart flow for when the Engineer needs to split work:

1. Engineer creates subtask(s) in Beads assigned to self
2. Engineer sends handoff mail to self (🤝 HANDOFF)
3. Engineer sends restart request to Witness
4. Witness verifies:
   - Handoff mail exists in Engineer outbox/sent
   - Subtasks filed in Beads
5. Witness restarts the Refinery session (new Engineer context)

This enables "occasionally consistent, eventually convergent" work patterns.
The Refinery continues; the Engineer gets fresh context.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:48.22994-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T23:07:42.05363-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-njr","depends_on_id":"gt-h5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:56.148564-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-nriy","title":"Test: Alpha to Beta","description":"Sibling communication test","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"message","assignee":"gastown-beta","created_at":"2025-12-20T21:44:00.731578-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T21:44:00.731578-08:00","labels":["thread:thread-d94865313b74"],"sender":"Steve Yegge","wisp":true}
 {"id":"gt-nti8","title":"Polecats should not push branches to remote","description":"## Current Behavior

Polecats push their branches to origin (e.g., `polecat/furiosa`), which pollutes the remote with many short-lived branches.

## Desired Behavior

Polecats should only commit locally. The Refinery handles all remote pushes:
1. Polecat works on local `polecat/<name>` branch
2. Polecat signals done (state → idle)
3. Refinery pulls from local polecat branch
4. Refinery runs tests, merges to main
5. Refinery pushes main to remote
6. If PR review needed, Refinery creates the PR

## Benefits

- Clean remote (no branch pollution)
- Clear responsibility (Refinery is the quality gate)
- Simpler cleanup (local branches deleted with worktree)
- Less noise in GitHub UI

## Trade-offs

- If polecat crashes before Refinery merges, code is lost locally
- But beads issue remains open, another polecat can redo the work
- This is acceptable for ephemeral workers

## Implementation

In polecat CLAUDE.md or landing protocol:
- Remove `git push origin HEAD` from workflow
- Replace with just `git commit` + signal done
- Refinery handles the rest","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:13:48.804954-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:13:48.804954-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-nz6t","title":"Remove unused style helper functions","description":"internal/style/style.go defines RenderSuccess, RenderWarning, RenderError, and RenderInfo helper functions that are never used. Code uses style.Success.Render() directly instead. Either use the helpers consistently or remove them.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:34:43.822193-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:50:45.224202-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:50:45.224202-08:00","close_reason":"Removed 4 unused style helper functions (RenderSuccess, RenderWarning, RenderError, RenderInfo)"}
